authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 98 - Child Care and Development Fund
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (U.S. Department of Health and Human Services)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 98 is the primary federal child-care program rule set and is authoritative for the child-care operating baseline, staff-support context, and regulated setting in which curriculum-support work occurs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program-context validation and child-care operating baseline

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 1302.32 - Curricula
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation directly governs research-based curricula, alignment to the Head Start Early Learning Outcomes Framework, curriculum fidelity monitoring, and support or feedback for implementation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: curriculum intake, implementation support, and fidelity review

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 1302.33 - Child screenings and assessments
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation is authoritative for how observation, screening, and assessment evidence may be used in early-childhood services and individualization, preventing curriculum-support work from overstating child-development conclusions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: assessment linkage and individualization evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 1302.34 - Parent and family engagement in education and child development services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation is authoritative for family-facing education and child-development communication, which directly constrains family-service and stakeholder outputs in curriculum-support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family-facing curriculum communication and home-learning support

authoritative_sources.title: 1303 Subpart C - Protections for the Privacy of Child Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This privacy rule is authoritative for disclosure controls, parental rights, and record maintenance governing child portfolios, assessment artifacts, and family-facing curriculum materials.

authoritative_sources.title: Standardized Training Procedures and Curriculum Materials to Support Implementation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official Head Start implementation guidance translates curriculum-support obligations into practical support artifacts such as lesson-plan supports, implementation materials, and staff training procedures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: support execution, material readiness, and coaching follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Developmentally Appropriate Practice in Early Childhood Programs Serving Children from Birth Through Age 8, Fourth Edition
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Association for the Education of Young Children
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAEYC's DAP position statement is a recognized early-childhood standard for curriculum planning, observation, family partnership, and assessment-informed teaching, making it a credible control baseline for non-regulatory workflow design.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: curriculum planning, observation use, and family partnership controls

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and curriculum-context validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the program model, approved curriculum, classroom age band, request intent, and systems-of-record references before treating the work as resolvable.

workflow_stages.name: Implementation artifact and support review
workflow_stages.description: Review lesson plans, scope and sequence, classroom observations, material inventories, and training or coaching artifacts to determine what support action is documented and what remains blocked.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Assessment, individualization, and family-communication control
workflow_stages.description: Check whether child screening or assessment artifacts and family-facing curriculum communication are sufficient and privacy-safe for the requested output.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception routing, repeat-failure escalation, and closure
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported exceptions, repeated support breakdowns, privacy-sensitive asks, and adjacent-lane ownership changes while preserving artifact traceability.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: approved curriculum lists, curriculum scope and sequence, and lesson plans
classroom observations, coaching feedback, and curriculum-fidelity checklists
material inventories, kit requests, and classroom setup or learning-environment support records
child portfolios, screening records, assessment summaries, and individualization notes
family curriculum communication logs, home-learning materials, conference notes, and translation or accessibility requests
escalation packets, blocker logs, and disposition summaries

allowed: Classify curriculum-support work as in-scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented artifacts and authority boundaries.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, stakeholder summaries, and escalation packets without claiming instructional or regulatory final authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Conflicting public guidance and tenant curriculum policy that materially change the support answer.
Requests requiring educational leadership approval, developmental diagnosis, disability determination, licensing interpretation, or privacy signoff.
Repeated support failures where blocker history shows the issue is not being resolved in the current lane.
refuse: Approve major curriculum adaptations, policy waivers, or curriculum replacements without the authorized review path.
Disclose child portfolios, assessment details, or family records without the required privacy and tenant context.
Treat curriculum support as generic service work that ignores child-care-specific curriculum, observation, assessment, and family-communication artifacts.

domain_failure_modes: curriculum-support work proceeds without knowing which curriculum version, age band, or lesson artifacts govern the classroom
child assessment or screening data is treated as diagnostic authority instead of bounded curriculum-support evidence
family-facing curriculum communication shares protected child details without clear privacy and tenant authorization context
material shortages, classroom setup issues, or coaching gaps are answered as if the lane owned facilities, procurement, or leadership approval
generic support language replaces curriculum-specific records such as scope and sequence, fidelity checklists, child portfolios, and individualization notes
repeat curriculum-support failures are retried indefinitely instead of escalated with blocker history and next-owner accountability

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific curriculum approval, observation, and record-retention rules outside the federal baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These rules can materially narrow classroom observation, approved-curriculum, and family-record workflows, so tenant retrieval is required before jurisdiction-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific boundaries between curriculum support, instructional coaching, and education-manager approval
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Ownership for coaching feedback, adaptation approval, and classroom materials may vary by operator, so the lane must ask for the tenant ownership map rather than guessing.

notes: The source set was selected to keep the pack grounded in real child-care curriculum records, family communication duties, and privacy constraints rather than generic support behavior.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant-specific and jurisdiction-specific differences are explicitly modeled as retrieval prerequisites and escalation triggers.
